name: United States (1890–1891)
description: Thirteen alternating red and white horizontal stripes (seven red, six white). In the upper hoist, a blue canton bearing 43 white five-pointed stars in six rows of 8-7-7-7-7-7.
history: Adopted on 4 July 1890 following the admission of Idaho, Montana, North Dakota, South Dakota, and Washington as states. It was the shortest-lived official star arrangement, replaced on 4 July 1891 after the admission of Wyoming brought the count to 44.

features.symbolism: the union of the states; a new constellation
the 43 states of the Union as of 4 July 1890
